name: Norepinephrine (noradrenaline)
executiveSummary: Sympathomimetic. Cardiogenic or septic shock with refractory hypotension and low peripheral resistance.
indications:
## Main indications

Sympathomimetic. Cardiogenic or septic shock with refractory hypotension and low peripheral resistance.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Ampoule or vial for IV infusion per institutional presentation.
adulto.reconstitution: Reconstitute per prescribing information; dilute in 5% dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
adulto.dose: Usual starting dose 0.05–0.1 mcg/kg/min; titrate to target MAP (prescription).
adulto.administration: Continuous IV infusion pump; central line preferred.
adulto.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
adulto.notes: Monitor HR, invasive BP, urine output, and peripheral perfusion.

pediatrico.finalConcentration: Usual 4 mcg/mL. With fluid restriction, 16 mcg/mL.
pediatrico.dose: 0.05-1 µg/kg/minute, maximum dose: 2 µg/kg/min Adults: start 4 µg/minute, Infusion: 8-12 µg/minute
pediatrico.notes: Oxidizes rapidly, do not use if brown discoloration is present. Dilution in normal saline is not recommended. Effects of norepinephrine may be increased in the presence of tricyclic antidepressants, antihistamines, and beta blockers.

neonatal.presentation: Ampoule or vial for IV infusion per institutional presentation.
neonatal.reconstitution: Reconstitute per prescribing information; dilute in 5% dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
neonatal.dose: 0.05–1 mcg/kg/min per NICU protocol and weight.
neonatal.infusionRate: Titrate according to BP and perfusion
neonatal.administration: Continuous IV infusion pump; central line preferred.
neonatal.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
neonatal.notes: Monitor HR, invasive BP, urine output, and peripheral perfusion.

stability:
## General

- Protect from light. Change solution per service policy (usually 24 h).

## Pediatric guide

- Discard remainder once opened. Dilution stable 24 h at room temperature.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Hypertension, necrosis, bradycardia. Prolonged use: decreased cardiac output, plasma volume depletion, severe peripheral and visceral vasoconstriction. Additionally, stress cardiomyopathy.
